market.advice: Confirmed uptrend, normal position sizing. Leadership is broad across sectors — buy strength on pullbacks, don't chase extended names at the highs.

picks.thesis: My top call today. Rails breaking out just 0.3% off its 52-week high with the calmest RSI in the group (58.9) — that combination means a clean entry that isn't extended, backed by 26% EPS growth. This is the one I'd buy at market rather than waiting.
strengths: Breaking out with RSI in the sweet spot (not overbought)
26% EPS growth confirms the move fundamentally
risks: Rails are economically sensitive — watch for any macro/freight-volume softness
R:R ~2.5:1, below my 3:1 ideal — size accordingly
watch: A close back below the breakout pivot would void the setup

picks.thesis: Best fundamentals in the group (33% EPS growth) and a textbook semi-cap leader at new highs — but RSI 74.7 is hot. I'm tagging this WATCH rather than BUY: wait for a pullback toward the breakout pivot before adding, don't chase it here.
strengths: 33% EPS growth — strongest in today's leaders
Semi-cap equipment is a primary AI-capex beneficiary
risks: RSI ~75 — overextended, prone to a shakeout if you chase the high
watch: A tight 2-4% pullback that holds the pivot is the entry trigger

picks.thesis: The only top name still building a base — 2.5% below its high with RSI 61, so it has room to run without being extended. Constructive, but it hasn't triggered yet. I want to see a volume breakout to a new high before committing.
strengths: Not extended — RSI 61 leaves upside room
risks: Hasn't broken out yet — could stall in the base
watch: New 52-week high on above-average volume = the buy signal

picks.thesis: Strongest earnings growth of the five (+37%) and at new highs, but RSI 67 sits near the top of the healthy band. Off-price retail is a solid defensive-growth angle — I'd add only on a tight pause, not at the current high.
strengths: +37% EPS growth — best of the group
Off-price model is resilient in a softer consumer
watch: A few quiet days near the high before the next push

picks.thesis: Defensive-growth beverage leader at new highs with 29% EPS growth — a good portfolio stabilizer. But like AMAT it's extended at RSI 72. Buy the pullback, keep the 8% stop tight given how far it's run.
strengths: 29% EPS growth in a defensive name
Lower beta — steadies a momentum-heavy book
risks: Lower R:R relative to entry up here
watch: Pullback toward the breakout pivot for a lower-risk entry

market_news.title: Semiconductor capex outlook lifts equipment names as AI buildout continues
market_news.summary: Analysts raised 2026 capex estimates, supporting semi-cap leaders like AMAT.

market_news.title: Rails post strong volume data; industrials broaden market leadership
market_news.summary: Freight volumes firm, underpinning CSX's breakout and a broadening rally.

market_news.title: VIX stays contained as SPY holds above key averages
market_news.summary: Low volatility keeps the uptrend intact; conditions favor normal position sizing.
